description: The Annotate Tool, available under the sidebar panel, allows users to add or correct map features and layers using both manual drawing tools and AI-assisted segmentation.
To begin, click on the Annotate option in the sidebar panel.
The Annotate tool follows a three-step workflow to create and manage annotation layers:
The Layer Configuration step allows you to create a new annotation layer or continue working on an existing one.
Create New: Enter a unique Annotation Layer Name and click 'Continue to Source' to proceed to Source Configuration.
Select Existing: Continue working with an existing annotation layer. Two data sources are available:

subdescription: Select the prediction dataset from the 'Select Prediction Result' dropdown.
Click 'Continue to Source' to load the prediction layer.
Select the vector dataset from the 'Select Dataset' dropdown.
Select the Target Class Field (attribute column).
The available classes from the selected attribute field will be used while creating the annotation layer.

description: Select one of the below imagery source that will be used for annotation.

subdescription: Choose one of the available basemaps such as Hybrid, Streets, Terrain or OSM.
Choose the required raster layer either from the dropdown or directly from the map.

description: Create the classes that will be used during annotation.
Enter the Class Name.

description.subdescriptionTitle: Select the Geometry Type for the class:

description: Choose color for the class from the color dot from the New Class Name dropdown.
Click 'Add' or press Enter to add the class.
Repeat the process to create multiple classes.
All created classes are displayed under Active Classes.
Each class displays its assigned geometry type and can be removed using the Delete icon.
The Project, Workspace, Layer and Source information is displayed at the bottom for reference.
Click 'Back' to return to the previous step and modify the configuration if required.
Click 'Save All' to create the annotation layer.
After saving the annotation layer, the Annotation Workspace opens.
The left panel displays the Annotation Layer Name, Workspace Name and total number of classes.
All created classes are listed under their respective geometry type.
Use the '+' icon to add new classes.
Use the Visibility icon beside the class to show or hide geometries of that class.
The selected class becomes active for drawing.
The active drawing class is also displayed at the bottom of the panel.
The 'Clear' button clears the currently selected class and its geometry.
GeoAI Copilot can be enabled from the bottom of the panel to perform AI-assisted annotation.
The Geometry toolbar provides annotation editing tools.
Draw Mode - Click on the map to draw new feature.
Select / Edit mode — click existing features to select, modify vertices, split, merge or delete
Recenter to Base Layer - Returns the map to the default layer extent.
Split Tool - Split a polygon into multiple features.
Merge Tool - Merge selected polygons into one feature.
Undo - Revert the last action.
Redo - Restore the last undone action.
Save - Save all annotation edits.
Delete - Delete the selected annotation.
Cancel - Exit the current editing operation.
Additional map controls are available on the right side of the workspace.
Comments - View and manage annotation comments.
Gallery - Browse all drawn features of all classes for the annotation layer.
Basemap - Switch between available basemaps.
Keyboard Shortcuts - View supported keyboard shortcuts for annotation.
GeoAI Copilot is an AI-powered annotation assistant that accelerates manual annotation.
Enable the GeoAI Copilot toggle to activate AI-assisted annotation tools.
Once active, click the arrow button to open the AI tools popover.

subdescription: select a class, then click anywhere on the map as per respective class the AI segments the object under your cursor and saves the polygon automatically.
Select an option from the toolbar below:
Draw: Draw reference feature on the map.
Magic Click: AI will draw reference feature.
The AI finds all similar objects in the area.

description: The copilot automatically detects and segments objects visible in the imagery.
Generated segments can be reviewed before assigning them to the required annotation class.
Users can accept, edit, merge, split or delete AI-generated polygons before saving.
GeoAI Copilot reduces manual effort while maintaining complete user control over the final annotations.
